title: Non-parameterized class takes parameters
body:
Hello!

If you use a type declared in a simple (non-parameterized) class as the type of a local parameter, then this same class can be specialized with any number and type of parameters.

Expected behavior is to throw an error.

Related (maybe) issue: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/5461

**Example:**

```systemverilog
module top;

  class cls1;
    typedef bit bool_t;
  endclass

  localparam cls1#(123, integer, "text")::bool_t PARAM = 1;

endmodule
```

**Output:**

Just normal output without error.

**Command:**

```bash
verilator --binary top.sv
```

---

Verilator version (git master): `Verilator 5.029 devel rev v5.028-88-g195a66529`

OS (verilator devcontainer): Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS
